sources.full: Raiders of the Serpent Sea: Player's Guide

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Provoked
entries: The practical Tvar spend long days in their fields, growing food and raising sheep, horses, and other livestock essential to sustaining the clans. Yet these homesteads are often isolated and an individual Tvar might be called upon to protect a sheep herd, farmhouse, or trapping cabin. Most learn at an early age how to fight effectively when outnumbered. Some among them take to this path with exceptional passion.

Though the farmers and smiths of the Tvar are often considered among the most cordial of the raiders, when pushed too hard, the Provoked barbarian retaliates swiftly and with no measure of mercy. Some who have witnessed such unleashed carnage swear they see two warriors, standing back-to-back like the god twins, Gnark and Idin.

subclassFeature.name: Strength of One
entries: Starting at 3rd level, while you are raging, your critical hit range increases by 1 for each hostile attacker within 5 feet, to a maximum equal to your proficiency bonus.
In addition, you gain one of the following features of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Dominant of Will
entries: At 6th level and beyond, you have learned to focus your will and in circumstances that might hinder a lesser hero, you always find a way to persevere.
You gain one of the following features of your choice (it can be different from your 3rd level choice).

subclassFeature.name: Violent Response
entries: Starting at 10th level, while you are raging, if a creature attacks or forces you to make a saving throw, you can use a reaction to mark that creature until the end of your next turn. While marked in this way, the creature is considered vulnerable to the next melee weapon attack made by you. If that attack hits, you deal maximum damage. You can use this ability once per rage.

subclassFeature.name: Reckoning of Choice
entries: Starting at 14th level, you have learned to turn a drawback into a powerful strike against your opponents. You gain one of the following features of your choice (it can be different from your previous choices).

subclassFeature.name: College of the Seers
entries: The ancient order of seers, the völv, examines the children of the raiding clans in search of individuals with the nascent ability to read the runes and connect with the primal forces of the world. Unfortunately, only a few have sufficient gifts to become seers. The others are sent back to their clans after being trained to compose music, sing songs, collect stories, and watch. Many become bards of the College of the Seers.

This bard is both a storyteller and a manipulator, capable of playing off both sides in a conflict and coming out the winner. Though skilled in the martial arts, they also know how to navigate the complexities of clan politics. And with a touch of the prescient, they can both understand what is to come and influence it.

The latter they do in the form of curses that weigh heavily on the minds of the afflicted, bending them to the skald's will (or by invoking blessings to benefit allies). Never entirely trusted, even by one's friends, this bard is always respected.

subclassFeature.name: Bestow Fate
entries: At 3rd level, as an action, you mark an individual that you can see or hear within 30 feet and utter a verbal curse against them. You expend one Bardic Inspiration die, assign it to the mark, and then select a fate effect for them. If the target fails a Charisma saving throw against your spell save DC, they are marked by the selected fate.

items.name: Clumsy
items.entry: You will reduce the first attack roll the mark makes each round by the result of a bardic die roll.

items.name: Feeble
items.entry: You will reduce the first saving throw the mark makes each round by the result of a bardic die roll.

items.name: Slow
items.entry: You will reduce the mark's movement speed by 5 feet multiplied by the result of a bardic die roll (a target's movement can never be reduced lower than 5 feet). You will reroll this result at the end of each of the mark's turns while the effect is active.

entries: After one minute, or if you mark a new target, this effect ends. The effect also ends if you or the target become {@condition unconscious}. When you use this feature to mark a new target any previous targets are no longer considered marked.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Starting when you swear your oath to the völv to join the College of the Seers at 3rd level, you embark on a lifelong journey, committed to the völv above all. You learn to protect the seers and have enhanced your ability to function as an effective agent for them. You gain proficiency with either martial weapons or medium armor. You also gain a skill of your choice.

subclassFeature.name: Enduring Inspiration
entries: Your curses may be used to empower (or goad) your allies into ever more daring escapades. Starting at 6th level, you can select two new fates for those marked by your Bestow Fate feature.

items.name: Bold
items.entry: You will increase the first ability check the mark makes each round by the result of a bardic die roll.

items.name: Sturdy
items.entry: You will increase the first saving throw the mark makes each round by the result of a bardic die roll.

entries: In addition, you can now apply Bestow Fate to more than one creature without it ending early so long as no two creatures share the same fate.

subclassFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can attack twice whenever you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Fate
entries: Starting at 14th level, if you are unhappy with the result of a Bestow Fate roll, you may reroll it and you must take the second roll. You may only do this once per turn. Additionally, you may use your reaction to force a target under the influence of your Bestow Fate to reroll a specific attack roll, saving throw, or ability check (in addition to the current fate effect). You must decide whether they will reroll after they have rolled but before the GM tells you of the result of their roll.

subclassFeature.name: Discovery Domain
entries: With their connections to the gods of the Stone Court, clerics are important contributors to raiding life. The Discovery Domain concerns itself with sailing, exploring, and seizing the riches to be found. These clerics often serve as raid captains or navigators and within each clan they are honored, respected, and at times, feared.
You have a favored god but respect them all and your divine power comes from the entirety of the Stone Court.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: At 1st level, you gain proficiency with martial weapons and {@item navigator's tools|PHB}. You also gain proficiency with vehicle (land) or vehicle (water).

subclassFeature.name: Eye of the Navigator
entries: Starting at 1st level, when you are traveling through any environment other than urban or dungeon, you have advantage on any Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) or Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks you make. Additionally, your experience at sea—and the blessing of your god—gives you a swim speed equal to your walking speed and you are able to hold your breath twice as long as normal.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Guided by the Divine
entries: Your years of exploration, combined with the bond you share with your god, grant you exceptional abilities to locate areas of interest. Starting at 2nd level, you may use an action to use your Channel Divinity to help locate a destination. Until your next long rest, you know the following information about any known location:

items: How far it is to your chosen destination in hours, days, or weeks.
The compass direction you need to take to reach your destination.

entries: You cannot determine the distance to a location that you have only heard of by story or myth, but you can determine the direction. If the location is not known by any mortal, the location is not on the same plane of existence, or is magically hidden, you learn neither direction nor distance, but this ability will confirm whether the destination actually exists.
You may only use this ability to focus on one location at a time—the most recent use of this feature overrides any previous uses.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Wrathful Flood
entries: Starting at 6th level, as an action you can use Channel Divinity to create a damaging wave of water that rapidly moves away from you. The wave may be up to 30 feet long, 10 feet high, and 10 feet wide and originates from a point within 60 feet of you that you can see.

Any creature caught in this area must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. If they fail, they take 17 ({@damage 5d6}) bludgeoning damage, are knocked {@condition prone} and {@condition restrained} until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and is not knocked {@condition prone} or {@condition restrained}. If this feature is used within 10 feet of any body of water, you may change the damage type to cold.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: At 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with divine energy. Once, on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} acid, cold, or lightning damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@dice 2d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Surveyor of the Depths
entries: Starting at 17th level, you can now breathe underwater, and your swim speed is doubled. Furthermore, your Wrathful Flood feature is enhanced. The wall persists and at the start of each of your turns it moves 10 feet away from you, carrying any creature that failed the previous round's saving throw with it. Any creature that enters (or starts their turn in) the wall must make the Strength saving throw or suffer the Wrathful Flood effects, except that the damage amount is changed.

items: {@b {@i First Round.}} 21 ({@damage 6d6}) damage.
{@b {@i Second Round.}} 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage.
{@b {@i Third round.}} 3 ({@damage 1d6}) damage and the wall disappears.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Devourer
entries: Under the deep, ever-stretching darkness of the long northern winters, the raiders of the Knattle clan have survived, even thrived, where no other dare. Here in this land of massive, shifting plains of ice and ever-howling snow squalls, druids have long connected with the primal world. They have isolated themselves on long pilgrimages to the highest rocky crags and they have raised their hands to the skies. And some rare few have borne witness to the ever-shifting lights of the aurora; some have touched the true essence of Grimnir.

You are one of these hardy, pragmatic druids. The few, the weak, must perish to nourish the many. And so, you have learned to wrestle the spirits of the lands to transform your own body in various, and often unpredictable ways. You become the defender when threatened, the hunter when starving, and the devourer when assaulted. Those spirits you dominate, though consumed, die to enable your survival, your community's survival, and ultimately the persistence of the world itself.
You ensure their sacrifice matters.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Protection
entries: Starting at 2nd level, the primal spirits exert their powers to protect you. If you are hit by an attack, you may use a reaction to roll {@dice 1d6} to determine a defensive effect:

items.name: (1-2) Protruding Spines
items.entry: Wickedly sharp spines protrude from your body, dealing piercing damage equal to 5 ({@dice 2d4}) + your Wisdom modifier to anyone within 5 feet of you. Any creature damaged in this way is also pushed 5 feet away from you. The spines then retract.

items.name: (3-4) Bulbous Shell
items.entry: Your skin hardens into a red shell mottled with blue smears. You immediately gain +3 to your Armor Class until the end of your next turn. If the increased Armor Class would have caused the current attack to miss, it does.

items.name: (5) Abyssal Glare
items.entry: Your eyes melt into orbs of glowing purple until the end of your next turn. During this time, anyone that hits you for damage—including the current attack—suffers double your Wisdom modifier in psychic damage.

items.name: (6) Aberrant Flesh
items.entry: Immediately after taking damage, you regain a number of hit points equal to 5 ({@dice 2d4}) plus your proficiency bonus.

entries: You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Form
entries: Starting at 6th level you may use a bonus action to expend a use of your Wild Shape feature to connect with a primal spirit and transform one arm into a fearsome weapon. This form lasts for 1 hour (or until dismissed as a bonus action).
You choose what natural weapon you adopt from the list below. These are considered simple weapons with which you are proficient, and you use your Wisdom modifier to calculate your attack bonus.
These attacks are considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

items.name: Bone Club
items.entry: Melee Weapon Attack: reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 ({@dice 1d8} + Wisdom modifier) bludgeoning damage. If damaged by the club, the target must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. If they fail, they are knocked {@condition prone}.

items.name: Bladed Claw
items.entry: Melee Weapon Attack: reach 5 ft., one target. Hit:  7 ({@dice 2d6} + Wisdom modifier) slashing damage.

items.name: Poisonous Barbs
items.entry: Ranged Weapon Attack: range 30 ft./60 ft., one target. Hit: 4 ({@dice 1d8} + Wisdom modifier) piercing damage. If damaged by a barb, the target must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. If they fail, they are {@condition poisoned} until the end of their next turn. While {@condition poisoned} in this way, a creature cannot regain hit points.

entries: Finally, when taking the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, you may make a second attack with your primal form weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Enhanced Senses
entries: Starting at 10th level, while in your Primal Form, you may use a bonus action to gain an additional sense. You may only have one of the following active at one time:

items: You gain {@sense darkvision} out to a range of 120 feet.
You gain {@sense blindsight} out to a range of 10 feet.
You gain {@sense tremorsense|MM} out to a range of 30 feet.

entries: This feature ends when your Primal Form ends.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Expertise
entries: At 14th level, you and your primal spirits share a near unbreakable bond and these powerful beings are even more eager to sacrifice themselves in your service. Your Primal Protection feature is restored after a short or long rest and instead of rolling for the Primal Protection result you select the result.

subclassFeature.name: Marauder
entries: All raiders seek glory in battle and the spoils that come from a life full of successful raids. Yet only a few live long enough to achieve these aims.
A marauder demonstrates leadership via small skirmishes at first and slowly they build upon their reputation. As their glory rises, so do their opportunities.

The marauder understands how to control any battlefield they find themselves upon, from the deck of a ship being tossed by a foul storm to the wind-tossed tundra of the north. They minimize their own losses while inflicting great damage upon their enemies.
A true leader leads by ferocious stubbornness and the marauder is no exception.

subclassFeature.name: Control the Battlefield
entries: Starting when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you may attempt to shove a creature as a bonus action, and if the creature you are trying to shove is already {@condition prone}, you automatically succeed on your check to shove them. In addition, when you shove a creature and choose to push them, you can push them up to 10 feet away instead of 5 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Withdraw
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you take damage from a melee attack, you may use a reaction to move 10 feet away from the attacker without provoking opportunity attacks. The attacker may then use their reaction to move 10 feet directly towards you. Their movement can provoke opportunity attacks as it normally would.

subclassFeature.name: Clear the Decks
entries: Starting at 7th level, you may use an action to strike the ground. All creatures within 5 feet must make a Strength saving throw against a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. A creature that fails the saving throw is pushed 10 feet from you and knocked {@condition prone}.

subclassFeature.name: Rampage
entries: Starting at 10th level, whenever an allied creature within 10 feet of you (this includes you) reduces a creature to 0 hit points with an attack, they may take a bonus action to move up to half their speed and make another attack.

subclassFeature.name: Enhanced Rampage
entries: Starting at 15th level, your Rampage feature's effective range widens to 30 feet. Additionally, any affected allies may roll a {@dice d6} and add the result to any attack rolls they make.

subclassFeature.name: End the Threat
entries: Starting at 18th level, you use a bonus action to shout a command to your companions to attack a specific target. Any allies within 30 feet of you that hear you may use their reaction to move half of their movement and make an attack (spell, ranged, or melee) against the target you specified.
You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Wanderer
entries: There is only ever one Wanderer at any given time, a novice always inheriting the mantle from their teacher when the time is right. The Wanderer travels the world of Grimnir, exploring its mysteries, and creating some of their own. And the Wanderer's actions, they have consequences. Their choices impact those around them... and often they must take great strides to maintain balance. A good deed in one village might ultimately cost another village elsewhere.

entries.name: From All Walks of Life
entries: The Wanderer does not need to come from the villages of the raiders. It is just as likely that a disgruntled Baendur knight or a merchant's child might have started following the Wanderer, learning the monk's ways, and preparing for the day when they replace their teacher. The world is theirs and as such, the borders of mortals mean little to them.

subclassFeature.name: Hands of Change
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you hit a hostile creature with an unarmed strike you can change the damage type. Whenever you do so, you can add an additional {@damage 1d4} damage of the chosen damage type to the damage dealt. Additionally, the weather within 1 mile of you changes based on your decision. These changes last for 1 hour.

entries.name: Using the Table
entries: Once per turn, when you use Hands of Change, adjust your Path of Fate score by the indicated amount in the table.

entries.caption: Hands of Change Table
rows: Reduce Rainfall or Reduce Snowfall or Reduce Wind

entries: The game master adjusts the temperature, wind, and precipitation on a table they have available to them.

At 6th level, your unarmed strike ignores any resistance to the chosen damage type (does full damage). At 17th level, your unarmed strike now treats the target's immunity as if it were resistance (strike does half damage).

subclassFeature.name: Path of Fate
entries: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you become a force in the world, ever growing in power. Your disposition influences those around you. Different effects arise depending on whether you are creating mayhem or restoring order. At the end of your turn, your Path of Fate Score determines your current Fate Effect, replacing your prior one.
You begin In Balance.

items.name: Disciplined Fortune
items.entry: Anyone within 30 feet of you (including yourself), rolls critical hits on a roll of 18 or higher.

items.name: Bear the Burden
items.entry: You may use a reaction to allow a target within 100 feet to ignore all damage from a single attack. You take this damage instead. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

items.name: In Balance
items.entry: You may reroll any die roll and decide which result you will use. You may do this a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and must then finish a long rest before using again.

items.name: Malicious Parry
items.entry: You may use a reaction to ignore all damage delivered to you from a single attack. A random NPC within 1 mile takes the damage instead. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

items.name: Misfortune of Chaos
items.entry: Anyone within 30 feet of you (including yourself) who rolls a 3 or under for an attack roll is considered to have suffered a critical miss.

subclassFeature.name: Everchanging Fate
entries: Starting at 6th level, you gain several new abilities that can be activated by spending Ki. These abilities either derive from calm and order or result from chaos and mayhem.

items.name: Final Punch [Ki Cost: 5].
items.entry: As a bonus action, after you have hit an opponent, you may attempt to banish them. If the target fails a Charisma saving throw against your Ki save DC, the target is banished as per the {@spell banishment} spell. After 1 minute has passed, the target reappears in the space it left or in the nearest unoccupied space if that space is occupied.

items.name: Twist of Fate [Ki Cost: 3].
items.entry: As a reaction, you may change a lethal melee attack that occurs within 5 feet of you to be non-lethal. An attack that would normally kill a target merely drops that target to zero hit points instead and they are {@condition unconscious} but stable.

items.name: Resist Order Damage [Ki Cost: 1].
items.entry: Gain resistance to Cold, Poison, or Radiant damage for one minute.

entries.name: The Cost of Order
entries: Using an Order ability decreases your Path of Fate by one.

items.name: Lightning Leap [Ki Cost:
items.entry: 5]. You move from your current location to another location within 30 feet without spending movement or provoking opportunity attacks. Any unarmed strikes you make during this turn inflict an additional {@damage 1d6} lightning damage, each.

items.name: Steal Fate [Ki Cost:
items.entry: 3]. You may select another creature in the current combat and take their place in initiative order (and they take yours).

items.name: Resist Mayhem Damage [Ki Cost:
items.entry: 1]. Gain resistance to Acid, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage for one minute.

entries.name: The Price of Mayhem
entries: Using a Mayhem ability increases your Path of Fate by one.

entries.name: Spending Ki
entries: Order abilities Ki cost is reduced by a number equal to your positive Path of Fate Score (maximum of 3). Mayhem abilities Ki cost is reduced by a number equal to your negative Path of Fate Score (maximum of 3). These abilities cannot cost less than 1 Ki.

items.name: Example:
entries: You have +3 to your Path of Fate and want to use Twist of Fate. Normally this costs 3 Ki, but based on your Path of Fate score, the cost is reduced to 1 Ki.

subclassFeature.name: Wandering Feet
entries: Starting at 11th level, you may use a bonus action and spend two Ki points to switch places with any ally your size or smaller within 5 feet. This movement costs no movement for either you or the ally and does not provoke opportunity attacks.
You also have the following additional movement speeds and types depending on the weather:

items: {@b While moving through heavy rain.} You gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed.
{@b While moving through heavy snowfall.} {@quickref difficult terrain||3} is considered normal terrain and you gain 10 feet to your walking speed. You also do not provoke opportunity attacks.

{@b While strong wind is blowing.} You may use an action to change the direction the wind is blowing. This may allow you to influence the direction your ship or other vessels are moving in, or help dissipate a mist, fog, or cloud. The moment you use any of your movement, the wind begins to blow from its original direction.

subclassFeature.name: Grace of the Wanderer
entries: Starting at 17th level, your Path of Fate score provides additional abilities. Using a bonus action, you may activate one of the following abilities:

items.name: Graceful Save
items.entry: Spend 2 Ki points. For one minute any {@dice d20} roll by you or an ally within 30 feet of you that is 9 or lower becomes a 10. You may only use this ability if your Path of Fate score is equal to or greater than +3.

items.name: Graceful Deter
items.entry: Spend 1 Ki point. You make an extra unarmed strike. On a successful hit, you knock an item held by the opponent out of their hand and it lands up to 15 feet away from them in a space of your choosing.

items.name: Graceful Strike
items.entry: If you hit an opponent with an attack, spend 1 Ki point to deliver bonus damage. Roll your weapon damage four times and deliver this total as additional Lightning, Thunder, Acid, Poison, or Fire (your choice) damage. You may only use this ability if your Path of Fate score is equal to or less than -3.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of the Slain
entries: The Slain are paladins that have embraced their inevitable death, emulating their Vanir hero, Thonir, and intending to serve him in their afterlife. They enforce clan law and often serve as executioners, ridding the raiders of criminals and the corrupt. The Slain believe the laws they follow are above those of the living.

Those that take the Oath of the Slain often paint their faces white and their tattoos often resemble death wounds. Primarily loners, the order meets rarely, usually only during the Gathering at Drifthall every eight years, where they exchange knowledge and take on apprentices.
However, though unusual, it is not unheard of for a few members of the order to temporarily band together to take on a larger threat.

entries.name: Other Beliefs
entries: Though paladins of other realms are welcome to explore Grimnir, work with your game master to ensure they fit the flavor of the world. The raiders can be a brutal people and a truly righteous paladin may struggle to find their place in this world.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity
entries: When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain the following two Channel Divinity options.

entries.name: Fight to the Last
entries: Immediately after you damage an opponent you may use your Channel Divinity as a bonus action to heal creatures of your choice within 15 feet of you. You may distribute hit points equal to twice your level in this class among the creatures you've chosen. If a creature was at 0 hit points when you use this feature on them, this healing effect is doubled for them.

entries.name: Mark of the Executioner
entries: As a bonus action you can use your Channel Divinity to mark an enemy for execution. For the next minute, whenever you attack this marked creature, you score a critical hit on an 18 or higher.

subclassFeature.name: Oath Spells
entries: Starting when you swear your oath at 3rd level, you gain Oath Spells at the listed levels and always have them prepared.

entries.name: Glory
entries: You desire to prove your worth to the gods and undertake dangerous endeavors to gain glory to honor them and ensure you are worthy of standing at their side in the afterlife.

entries.name: Incorruptible
entries: The laws of Grimnir are above all other things, including friends and relationships. You know what is right or wrong and it is your duty to enforce a law in a righteous but fair manner.

entries.name: Death is the Beginning
entries: Dying only transforms you into a greater being to serve the will of the gods.

entries.name: Prepare for the End
entries: Wealth is meant to be spent to better Grimnir and its peoples, and for preparing them against the inevitable end of the world.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of the Executioner
entries: Starting at 7th level, you always emanate an aura that grants you and your allies increased focus when attacking enemies. You, and any allies within 10 feet of you, may add {@dice 1d4} to all attack rolls made each turn. This benefit ends if you become {@condition incapacitated}.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 20 feet and the attack roll bonus increases to {@dice 1d6}.

subclassFeature.name: A Glorious Sacrifice
entries: Starting at 15th level, your dedication prevents you from dying easily and if you die, no spell, magical effect, or other feature can ever make you return to life as an undead creature. Additionally, after taking damage that reduces you to 0 hit points and does not kill you outright, you may use your reaction to temporarily thwart death and remain conscious. You immediately take an extra turn, interrupting the current turn, but can only take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action as well as any movement required to reach an enemy to attack. The movement provided by this feature does not provoke opportunity attacks.

You are considered to have failed one death saving throw by activating this feature and while you have 0 hit points, damage against you causes you to fail another death saving throw (three death saving throw failures will kill you). When the extra turn ends, you fall unconscious if you still have 0 hit points.
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Justice Delivered
entries: Starting at 20th level, you are grimly determined to have a death worthy of the greatest of heroes. You may use an action to undergo a transformation that lasts for 1 minute (or until you are {@condition incapacitated} or killed). During this time you have the following benefits:

items.name: Impervious Flesh
items.entry: You gain resistance to all damage.

items.name: Of One Mind
items.entry: You are immune to being {@condition charmed} and any allies within 30 feet have advantage on saving throws against charm effects.

items.name: Might of the Gods
items.entry: When an ally within 10 feet hits an enemy with a melee attack, you may use your reaction to attack that enemy (if you are in range) with advantage.

entries: Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Wolf Rider
entries: In the deep snow-covered forests, the clan of the Whar has existed since Grimnir's founding. Decades ago, though capable sailors, their skill in battle shone brightest when astride the great horses that formed their cavalry. For generations they were unstoppable, though many credited their worship of the goddess, Hel, for their successes. They were feared, both among the raiders and the other peoples of the world.

Yet over the past few generations their victories have dwindled, as have their numbers. Feeding their herds became impossible and many of their youth found their way to sea, to other clans. They eventually lost their seat in the inner council.

But change is afoot in the world of Grimnir again. And it all began with the wolves. These creatures, mere legends from the Lost Lands, have reappeared. Gifted, so it appears, by the Lady of the Dead to her most faithful of worshipers, the Whar. The wolves have upset the existing clan hierarchy because those the wolves select as partners are instantly elevated in the clan leadership. Newer voices are being heard, as these wolf riders take their place.

"The wolf decides," is a saying often uttered. You, as a wolf rider, seek glory for a re-emerging Whar clan, and desire to honor Hel, the Lady of the Dead, with heroic deeds. You are part of a pack, destined to never fight alone.

subclassFeature.name: Bonded
entries: At 3rd level, your wolf and you share a limited telepathy, with a range of 120 feet and it can communicate its feelings and intent to you. Using a bonus action, you can command your wolf to take the following actions:

items: The {@action Attack|PHB}, {@action Disengage|XPHB}, {@action Dash|XPHB}, or {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

The {@action Dodge|PHB} action. In addition to its usual effects, when your wolf takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action, it helps to confuse opponents attacking you. Any attack rolls against you are now made with disadvantage until the start of your next turn (as long as the wolf remains within 10 feet of you).

The {@action Help|PHB} action. When you command your wolf to use a {@action Help|XPHB} action to assist you in combat against a specific creature, you improve your chance of scoring a critical hit. Any roll of 18 or higher is now a critical hit.
Actions from special abilities, as described below.

subclassFeature.name: Wolf Companion
entries: At 3rd level, a {@creature young Grimnir wolf|RaidersSerpentSea} companion finds you. The wolf obeys your commands to the best of its abilities and acts on your initiative in combat. It can move on its own and take reactions but can only take an action if you use a bonus action to command it (see the Bonded ability, below). If you are {@condition incapacitated}, the wolf can use actions to defend you.

Your wolf uses its stat block but has hit points equal to 15 plus 5 ({@dice 1d10}) per ranger level, and adds your proficiency bonus to its Armor Class, saving throws, attack rolls, and damage rolls. The wolf will regain any lost hit points during a short or long rest. If your wolf is ever slain, you must bury it as soon as is convenient. It emerges from the grave, restored to full health, after you finish a long rest.

For purposes of the wolf's Pack Tactics ability, only you count as the wolf's ally. The wolf also gains the benefits of your Land's Stride ability and can move stealthily at a normal pace when traveling within 10 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Woodland Rider
entries: At 5th level your wolf grows into an {@creature adult Grimnir wolf|RaidersSerpentSea} and can now be used as a mount (and does not require a saddle). The time spent training together has also made the two of you more effective in combat. While within 5 feet of one another you both gain a bonus of +1 to AC (as long as neither is {@condition incapacitated}). Additionally, while mounted, the following benefits apply:

items: Whenever an attack misses you or your wolf, you or your wolf can use a reaction to move the wolf up to half its speed without either of you provoking an attack of opportunity.
Mounting or dismounting costs 10 feet of movement (not half your speed) and you have advantage to avoid falling off your mount.
You and the wolf have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

subclassFeature.name: Pack Leader
entries: At 7th level your training continues. Your wolf and you become more adept at fighting together. Your wolf's attacks now count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
You also have the following new bonus actions:

items.name: Wall of Fur
items.entry: Use your wolf to grant you {@quickref Cover||3||total cover} (can't be targeted directly by an attack or a spell) until the beginning of your next turn. This ends early if you and your wolf are ever more than 5 feet apart.

items.name: Combat Assist
items.entry: Your wolf uses its {@action Help|XPHB} action to assist you in combat and, if you hit, the wolf may use a reaction to make a free bite attack against that same creature.

items.name: Near Shot
items.entry: You do not suffer disadvantage on ranged weapon attacks, even when within 5 feet of an opponent (as long as your wolf is within 10 feet of the target.)

subclassFeature.name: Dangerous Beast
entries: At 11th level your wolf grows into a {@creature Grimnir frost wolf|RaidersSerpentSea} or {@creature Grimnir forest wolf|RaidersSerpentSea} (your choice).

entries.name: Lethal Pack
entries: If your wolf is using its {@action Help|XPHB} action to aid you in combat, your chance to score a critical hit improves to a roll of 17 or higher. Alternatively, if you instead use your {@action Help|XPHB} action to help the wolf fight, your wolf scores critical hits on a roll of 15 or higher.

subclassFeature.name: Defend the Pack
entries: Starting at 15th level, if you or your wolf scores a critical hit against an enemy, you may move up to half your movement and use your reaction to attack another target within range. This feature does not expend your movement.

subclassFeature.name: True Believer
entries: This rogue has gotten themselves into too many dangerous situations. One evening, with their life on the line, they do the unthinkable. They pray to Mirgal, the dead Vanir whose treacherous plots doomed the Lost Lands. Just a quick prayer, a few words muttered under their breath in the most desperate of situations.

While desperately beseeching Mirgal for assistance... something happens. The rogue escapes with their life. And has never forgotten. They keep their faith secret but call upon Mirgal's assistance when it is most needed. But...  is Mirgal actually helping? Can a dead wizard even help... anyone? This rogue does not care... they are a true believer.

entries.name: Mirgal's Long Reach
entries: This archetype works for a Baendur-based character as well as a raider. Perhaps, in a moment of desperation, a Baendur lesser noble who pays for her lavish lifestyle by borrowing the baubles of other nobles, calls out for Mirgal, whose name and story she once heard from a captured raider...

subclassFeature.name: False Gods
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you have advantage on Intelligence ({@skill Religion}) checks to learn about other religious orders. As well, any Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check by someone trying to determine your true religion, has disadvantage. You also gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill History}, or {@skill Religion}.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you gain the ability to cast cleric spells.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You may learn three cantrips of your choice from the cleric's spell list.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The True Believer table determines the number of spell slots you have to cast spells from your known spells list. Casting a spell expends a slot of that spell's level or higher. All expended spells slots are restored after finishing a long rest.

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: The Spells Known column of the True Believer table shows when you learn new cleric spells. The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. Spells cast remain in your list of known spells. You may only expend spell slots to cast spells that you know.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence or Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your cleric spells (you choose this when you select this class). You use this ability whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use this ability's modifier when setting the saving throw DC for a cleric spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

subclassFeature.name: Dubious Healing
entries: Starting at 9th level, when you cast a healing spell on a target, you may choose to grant the target double the healed amount as temporary hit points (instead of actually healing them). These temporary hit points last until the target takes a short or long rest, or you revoke them with a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Wrath of Divinity
entries: Starting at 13th level, when you cause damage with your Sneak Attack, you may inflict an additional 10 ({@damage 3d6}) damage. Good and neutral characters deliver radiant damage and evil characters, necrotic. If you deal radiant damage, you may heal an ally within 60 feet for a number of hit points equal to the extra damage dealt with this feature. If you deal necrotic damage, the creature you hit must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.
You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You must then finish a long rest before using this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Push Your Luck
entries: Starting at 17th level, you may use an action to request divine assistance. If you succeed in a DC 15 ability check (using your spellcasting ability) you regain all expended spell slots and True Believer class features
If you fail the check, you gain one level of {@condition exhaustion}. But the very next time you attempt to use this ability, you have advantage on the ability check.
You may use this feature once. You regain all expended uses after you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Misplaced Soul
entries: It was said that the long dead trickster Mirgal was capable of creating magical beings so real that no one could tell that they were illusions. It was a favorite pastime of his to torment others with an imaginary lover, nemesis, or long-lost child, only to dispel the creature at his whim.

At some point in your life, you began to feel not entirely connected to the real world. You heard whispers from another plane and began to have conversations with what you initially assumed were imaginary companions. But over time you have begun to wonder if they are indeed real. Or if you, like them, are not.

Could you be an illusion conjured by a powerful magic user that has somehow been made real? Or do you simply have a connection with a powerful force beyond your understanding? The answers to these questions may be part of what drives you to search and to explore...

subclassFeature.name: Act as One
entries: You do not know if they are actually spirits or are indeed other {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls}, like yourself, that have not yet been made fully corporeal. Regardless, you have a connection with them, and can communicate telepathically with any of your {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} when they are within 120 feet of you.

Starting at 1st level, as a bonus action on your turn, you can move any number of your summoned {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} up to 30 feet. At the end of their movement, each summoned {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} can then take any available standard combat action or those from the {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} stat block, once they are available, or you can swap places with one of them instead. A swap costs no movement to either you or the {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} and does not trigger opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Companions from Before
entries: In your dreams you remember a vast and strange place that you have come to believe is where you originated—or where you were kept until needed. You have memories of other souls, like yourself, lingering in that domain.

At 1st level, you may use a bonus action to summon one of these creatures to a location within 10 feet of yourself. They may look like any small or medium sized creature you have seen. The total number of {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} you may summon at one time with this feature increases to 2 at level 6, 3 at level 14, and 4 at level 18. Summoned {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} disappear if either you or they are reduced to zero hit points, if you use a bonus action to dismiss any number of them, or after 10 minutes.
You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Unseen Allies
entries: The {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} are initially {@condition invisible}, but you may choose to reveal them to others at any time. Their features ripple and distort however as they move, making their otherness obvious to any viewer.

subclassFeature.name: Hinder and Help
entries: Your connection with your otherworldly companions grows stronger. Starting at 6th level you may now use a bonus action to spend sorcery points to empower them in various ways. This can also be done as part of the bonus action you use to summon a {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea}.

items: For 1 sorcery point, one {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} adds the Interfere feature to its stat block. Interfere. Melee Weapon Attack: your spell attack modifier to hit, reach 5 ft., one Medium or smaller creature. Hit: The creature is {@condition grappled} (escape DC is your spell save DC).

For 2 sorcery points one {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} gains the Misplaced Defender feature: they gain 5 times your sorcerer level in temporary hit points, a +4 bonus to their Armor Class, the Disrupt action, and the Reduce Harm reaction.

{@b Disrupt.} The {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} marks a creature within 60 feet and until the start of your next turn, that creature has its attack roll reduced by your proficiency bonus.

{@b Reduce Harm.} If a creature within 5 feet of the {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} is hit, the {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} can use its reaction to grant the target immunity to the damage dealt. The {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} takes the full amount of damage the target would have taken instead.

entries: At any time, a {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} may only have one special feature and each special feature may only be applied to one {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} (this includes the additional feature unlocked at level 14).

subclassFeature.name: The Imagined Offense
entries: Starting at 14th level, you gain an additional feature that can be granted to a {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} by using a bonus action:

items: For 2 sorcery points one {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea} gains the Violent Force feature. They gain 5 times your sorcerer level in temporary hit points, +2 to their Armor Class, and a multiattack action: they may use the Illusionary Rend attack and then two Violent Thrashing attacks.
{@b Illusionary Rend.} Ranged Weapon Attack (Requires Violent Force): your spell attack modifier to hit, range 30 ft., one target. Hit: 11 ({@damage 2d10}) + PB psychic damage.
{@b Violent Thrashing.} Melee Weapon Attack (Requires Violent Force): your spell attack modifier to hit, range 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 ({@damage 1d12}) + PB psychic damage.

entries: Additionally, you may use a bonus action to consume one or more {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} (your choice). You are healed by twice the combined total hit points of the consumed souls and they are destroyed. You regain one sorcery point.

subclassFeature.name: Dreams Made Real
entries: Starting at 18th level, you may use a bonus action to force any summoned {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls} to merge as long as they are within 60 feet of each other. You choose the combinations and the location of any merged {@creature misplaced soul|RaidersSerpentSea|misplaced souls}. Any temporary hit points that the merged souls had become actual hit points. The combined then add their hit points together, stack any Armor Class bonuses, and gain all actions and reactions from all of the combined souls. Additionally, depending on the number of souls combined, further features are available to this new creature.

items.name: 2 Souls
items.entry: The creature gains Magical Resistance: you have advantage on any saving throw against spells. You may only be damaged by magical weapons.

items.name: 3 Souls
items.entry: The creature is stronger and gains an additional +1 to its Armor Class and adds your Charisma modifier to all attacks rolls and to any damage it does. It gains damage resistance to all damage types.

items.name: 4 Souls
items.entry: The creature gains regeneration—as long as it begins its turn with at least one hit point, it regains 10 hit points.

entries: If you become {@condition incapacitated} or are reduced to zero hit points while the combined soul is present, roll a {@dice d20}. If you roll a 1 or 2 the combined soul becomes a real creature that persists. They are no longer under your control and have the stat block of a commoner. Their disposition is friendly towards you.

subclassFeature.name: The Ironwood
entries: Your patron is a powerful ironheart tree, a patriarch or matriarch of one of the great Ironwood forests. These trees originated from the old world, the Lost Lands. Their motives are unknown, not even to those who claim to have much knowledge of them. Regardless, and for better or worse, you have aligned yourself with them.

Perhaps you were stranded, lost, or otherwise abandoned in such a forest. Or you may have sought them out intentionally, eager to wield their ancient powers. Regardless, you have sworn yourself into the service of the Ironwood.
It is yet to be determined what this means for you—and for Grimnir.

subclassFeature.name: Vitality of the Ironwood
entries: At 1st level, your patron gifts you with a heartseed. It is  a Tiny object and may be used as your spellcasting focus for warlock spells. If the seed is destroyed or lost, you may perform a 1-hour ceremony to receive a replacement.

While you are touching the heartseed you may use a bonus action to imbue yourself with the Vitality of the Ironwood.  For 10 minutes you gain temporary hit points equal to 3 x your warlock level and your movement speed is increased by 5. The effects of this feature do not stack; if Vitality of the Ironwood is used while already active, the previous temporary hit points and movement speed increase end. This feature may be used a number of times per long rest equal to your proficiency bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: Your connection with the Ironwood Forest grants you access to an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Armor of the Ironwood
entries: Starting at 6th level, during a short rest you may grow a suit of armor from your heartseed. Only you may wear the armor and it lasts until you decide to grow a different suit. While wearing your Ironwood Armor your AC equals 10 + Dexterity modifier + Charisma modifier. You may still use a shield while wearing this armor.
The armor grown is one of the following (your choice):

items.name: Mantle of Leaves
items.entry: You have advantage when making {@skill Stealth} checks. While using your Vitality of the Ironwood feature you gain another 5 feet of movement.

items.name: Unbreakable Branches
items.entry: You have disadvantage on {@skill Stealth} checks. While wearing this armor you are immune to critical hits whenever your Vitality of the Ironwood feature is active.

entries: The heartseed can still be used as a spellcasting focus while transformed in this way.

subclassFeature.name: Heart of the Ironwood
entries: Starting at 10th level, while wearing your Armor of the Ironwood, you now have resistance to bludgeoning damage from non-magical/silvered weapons. Additionally, you may use 10 feet of movement to magically enter a tree within your reach. You know the location of all trees within 500 feet of you and their type. You must exit from one of these trees and will appear within 5 feet of it. If you enter a tree with an intelligence score you roll a number of {@dice d6} equal to your proficiency bonus. Your hit points are restored by this amount. If you enter any other type of tree, it explodes in a 15-foot radius. All creatures of your choosing in the area of effect must make a Dexterity saving throw. They suffer 17 ({@damage 5d6}) piercing damage on a failed saved, half that amount on a successful save. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Colossus of the Ironwood
entries: Starting at 14th level, you are able to enhance your Armor of the Ironwood, becoming a juggernaut of destruction. You may use an action to trigger the transformation: your armor rapidly expands in size with numerous roots and limbs erupting from it. Upon doing so, everyone of your choosing within 15 feet must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or suffer 17 ({@damage 5d6}) bludgeoning damage. The damage is halved on a successful saving throw.
While in this form you retain all your existing abilities (you can still cast spells and use weapons) and you also have the following traits:

items.name: Tree Slam
items.entry: You use the branches extending from you to make an unarmed strike. You use your spell attack modifier to hit, it delivers bludgeoning damage equal to 17 ({@dice 5d6}) + your Strength modifier + your Charisma modifier. This attack is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

items.name: Larger Size
items.entry: You count as Large size when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift. You also add 10 feet to your walking speed, 5 feet to your reach, and inflict double damage to objects and structures.

items.name: Protected
items.entry: You gain 140 ironwood hit points. Unlike temporary hit points, these hit points can be restored with healing. If these ironwood hit points are reduced to zero this feature ends early and any excess damage carries over to your normal hit points.

items.name: Resolute
items.entry: You add your Charisma modifier to all Strength saving throws. You gain resistance to piercing damage and vulnerability to fire damage.

items.name: Ironwood Shell
items.entry: As an action you become a wall of branches, protecting yourself and others. While active, you add your proficiency bonus to your Armor Class, you cannot be knocked {@condition prone}, and you have no movement. Any ally within 5 feet gains {@quickref Cover||3||half cover}. You may end the Ironwood Shell by using a bonus action.

entries: Unless it ends early, with an action or by other means, this feature lasts for 10 minutes. You must finish a long rest to use this feature again.

subclassFeature.name: Cohesion of the Primal
entries: In the traditions of many worlds, there is a belief in an early, primeval, state of the world. It is a belief that beings from this long ago can be harnessed, bargained with, or exploited by powerful spellcasters with varying degrees of success... and often tragic results. To this day some raider wizards still seek out the primal path, believing it alone might hold the secret to preventing what befell the Lost Lands from afflicting Grimnir. These are known as dreamers.

There are other wizards that have drawn a boundary between what is to be known and what must be forgotten. These seekers search the world, destroying ancient tomes and desecrating powerful artifacts—and killing those that hold onto such powers.
Among every clan there might be wizards of both persuasions, though dreamers often hide their true nature.

Not that it matters, for most raiders dislike any and all wizards, due to their association with the magic of Mirgal. Owing to this, most raider wizards are isolated and surly. Outside of the training of their apprentices, wizards only meet their peers at the octennial gathering of the clans. Otherwise, they seldom interact, except to battle one another. These wizards aggressively hunt for new magic and arcane advantage, the way other raiders raid for treasure.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Intentions
entries: You must decide why you wield arcane magic. You may be a dreamer trying to connect with the secret arcane mysteries of the world. These individuals seek to unravel all the primal mysteries of Grimnir, following in the path set by Mirgal but avoiding his mistakes. Dreamers read everything they find and will go to great lengths—and risk—to uncover any ruins or artifacts from the long ago.

You may decide instead to oppose the dreamers and stop them from unleashing the magic you fear may consume Grimnir as it did the Lost Lands. Your training with the arcane is devoted to seeking out and over-whelming other wizards.
At 2nd level, you select an intention to follow. At levels 6, 10, and 14, you may change and follow a different intention, losing access to your old powers.

Both dreamers and seekers gain proficiency with light armor. Seekers also gain proficiency with two melee weapons of their choice (if they ever become dreamers, they lose this proficiency until they become seekers again). In addition, each intention starts with a special ability.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Ink
entries: Writing is an uncommon practice for raiders and they often rely on oral traditions to pass knowledge from one generation to another. Wizards are among the few groups that use writing daily but given the limited access to writing material, primal wizards chose to ink their magic onto their very flesh.

Beginning when you select this school at 2nd level, you are considered proficient with the art of tattooing. Primarily you use this ability to scribe your spells into your flesh. To copy a new spell as a tattoo requires twice the time of copying a spell into a standard spellbook but half the cost.

During a long rest, your known spells swim across your skin, like auroras, allowing you to select which should be prepared. Once you have made your choices, the tattoos settle into runes of any color you prefer, and any spells not chosen for preparation withdraw deep into your flesh. The magical nature of the tattoos prevents them from being harmed, no matter the danger that befalls you.
However, you must have an arcane focus available to prepare your spells.

subclassFeature.name: Foresaying
entries: Those that study the primal have a way of seeing the connections between the past and the future, or between individuals. These connections are imagined as knotted cords and with experience, a primal wizard can see and understand many such connections and use this knowledge to further their own actions. At 6th level, you become able to influence the future, based on your understanding of the past.

subclassFeature.name: Turn of Fate
entries: At 10th level, your knowledge of the knotted cords has increased, better preparing you to weather the hazards of Grimnir.

subclassFeature.name: Cord & Curse
entries: By 14th level, both dreamers and seekers have gained tremendous expertise that serves them well, especially in trials of arcane combat.

optionalfeature.name: Accustomed to the Sky (-1 Point)
entries: Your experience as an animal grants you proficiency with Wisdom (Survival) checks (and you double your proficiency bonus for those checks) when in the air.

optionalfeature.name: Accustomed to the Water (-1 Point)
entries: Your experience as an animal grants you proficiency with Wisdom (Survival) checks (and you double your proficiency bonus for those checks) when underwater or within 1 mile of a body of water.

optionalfeature.name: Accustomed to the Wild (-1 Point)
entries: Your experience as an animal grants you proficiency with Wisdom (Survival) checks (and you double your proficiency bonus for those checks) while on land and not in cities or towns.

optionalfeature.name: Amphibious (-3 Points)
entries: You can breathe air and water.

optionalfeature.name: Arcane Intentions: Dreamer
entries: Your connection with the primal allows you to anticipate what may unfold for you on any given day, affording you more flexibility than other wizards. As a bonus action you may swap a prepared spell for any other spell of the same level or lower in your spellbook. You may do this a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier. You must then finish a long rest to use this feature again.

optionalfeature.name: Arcane Intentions: Seeker
entries: You are skilled with tracking down opponents and deciphering the secrets of their lairs. You gain proficiency with the {@skill Investigation} skill and your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability checks you make with the {@skill Investigation} skill. You may also use an action (and expend a spell slot) to sense every arcane wielder of magic within 1 mile of you and you know their relative direction in relation to where you currently are. This effect lasts for 2 hours, allowing you to track down all but the most elusive of enemies.

optionalfeature.name: Armored Flight (-7 Points)
entries: You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed even while wearing light or medium armor. You cannot fly in heavy armor.

optionalfeature.name: Awkward on Land (+3 Points)
entries: You are clumsy and your walking speed is only 20 feet. Any other movement options selected as part of building your beastborn are based on your original walking speed, not this reduced speed.

optionalfeature.name: Beast Grasp (-3 Points)
entries: This might be a bear hug or a python coiling around a foe. When you hit with your unarmed strike, you may use a bonus action to grapple the opponent (as long as they are no more than one size larger than you). Make a standard grapple check and if you win the opponent is considered grappled. Grappled foes can attempt to escape the grapple on their turn. The escape DC is 10 + your Strength (Athletics) modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Burrow (-4 Points)
entries: You have a burrowing speed equal to half your walking speed.

optionalfeature.name: Charge (-3 Points)
entries: If you move at least 30 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee weapon attack (on the same turn), you may use a bonus action to make one attack with your unarmed strike.

optionalfeature.name: Climb (-2 Points)
entries: You have a climbing speed equal to half your walking speed. If you purchase this feature a second time, your climbing speed is equal to your walking speed.

optionalfeature.name: Cold Resistance (-3 Points)
entries: You have resistance to cold damage.

optionalfeature.name: Cord & Curse: Dreamer
entries: You have become very proficient at working your way through the fate cords affecting you. When you fail a {@status concentration} check you may use your reaction to immediately cast another spell that requires concentration. You must have a valid spell available to cast and its normal casting time must be no longer than one action. If these conditions are met, you automatically succeed with the {@status concentration} check and are now maintaining both spells. Subsequent {@status concentration} checks are made with disadvantage while you are {@status concentration||concentrating} on both spells. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Cord & Curse: Seeker
entries: You have refined your ability to prevent rival spellcasters from unleashing deadly magic on the battlefield. When you damage a target with a spell, you can use a bonus action to afflict the target with a spellcurse. While afflicted with the curse, the target is not aware of it and the first time they attempt to cast any spell (including innate spellcasting) they must make a Wisdom save against your spell save DC. If they fail the check, the spell fails, and they lose the ability to cast that particular spell for the remainder of the day. You may use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier and you regain expended uses after a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Creature of the Depths (+3 Points)
entries: You can breathe air and water, but you must be submerged at least once every eight hours to avoid suffocating.

optionalfeature.name: Creature of the Trees (-3 Points)
entries: You use your Dexterity score to determine how far you can jump (instead of Strength). Additionally, when falling you reduce the fall distance by 30 feet. (That is, a 60-foot fall would only be a 30-foot fall for you; a fall of 30 feet or less would result in no damage.)

optionalfeature.name: Darkvision (-3 Points)
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You discern colors in the darkness only as shades of gray. You may purchase this feature multiple times to extend the range.

optionalfeature.name: Endurance (-4 Points)
entries: When you are reduced to 0 hit points (but not killed) you can drop to 1 hit point instead. Once you have used this trait you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

optionalfeature.name: Fearsome Bite (-2 Points)
entries: If you have grappled an opponent, you can perform a bite attack on them as a bonus action. You have advantage on the bite attack, which functions otherwise like a normal melee weapon attack and inflicts 1d6 piercing damage plus your Strength modifier if it hits.

optionalfeature.name: Fish Out of Water (+3 Points)
entries: You are clumsy and your walking speed is only 20 feet. Any other movement options selected as part of building your beastborn are based on your original walking speed, not this reduced speed.

optionalfeature.name: Foresaying: Dreamer
entries: Having some awareness of the future, you ensure you are always well prepared. If you spend 1 minute, you may cast any spell that targets only yourself, but save its activation for later. After having used this Foresaying ability, you may activate that spell at any time, as a bonus action.  You may only have one Foresaying spell active at a time and may only use your Foresaying ability a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier. After that you must finish a long rest to use this feature again. If you have not cast your Foresaying spell when you take a long rest, it is lost.

optionalfeature.name: Foresaying: Seeker
entries: You remove an enemy's power in the present by having them forget something important from their past. After hitting an opponent with a ranged or melee attack, you may use your bonus action to try to disrupt their spellcasting. If the target fails an Intelligence saving throw (with the DC equal to your spell save DC) they lose a prepared spell from their list. If they have cast a particular spell on a previous turn, you may pick that spell to be removed. Otherwise, the spell removed is selected at random:

items: Lower-level spells are removed before higher level spells.
Innate spellcasting is also affected. Lower power spells (such as 3/day) are removed before more powerful spells (such as 1/day).

optionalfeature.name: Glide (-3 Points)
entries: When you fall you can use a bonus action (or a reaction) to slow your movement to 60 feet per round. Your glide lasts for three rounds and if you land on your feet before this feature ends, you take no fall damage.

optionalfeature.name: Hold Breath (-1 Point)
entries: You can hold your breath for an additional 15 minutes before suffocating. You can purchase this feature multiple times.

optionalfeature.name: Keen Hearing (-1 Point)
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing.

optionalfeature.name: Keen Sight (-1 Point)
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

optionalfeature.name: Keen Snout (-1 Point)
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell.

optionalfeature.name: Leap (-3 Points)
entries: As an action, if your speed is greater than 0, you can jump a number of feet equal to 5 times your proficiency bonus. You do not provoke opportunity attacks when you use this feature. The jump made by this feature does not expend your movement.

optionalfeature.name: Long-limbed (-3 Points)
entries: When using a melee weapon your reach with that weapon is increased by 5 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Longer-lived (-1 Point)
entries: Your lifespan is that of a Human.

optionalfeature.name: Modified Unarmed Strike (-3 Points)
entries: You may use the claws, horns, teeth, or hooves of your original form to make unarmed strikes. You choose the appropriate damage type (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) when you select your animal of origin. When you hit, you inflict 1d6 damage of that type (+ your Strength modifier).

optionalfeature.name: Natural Armor (-4 Points)
entries: When you select your animal of origin, you must pick your natural armor. You might be bulky (or have thick fur or skin) in which case you have an AC of 17 but are unable to increase this with dexterity modifiers or armor. Or instead, you might be agile and have an AC of 13 + your Dexterity modifier (when you wear armor, you use the higher AC score; they do not stack). In either case you may still use a shield.

optionalfeature.name: Natural Dash (-2 Points)
entries: When you take a {@action Dash} action you may elect to run on all fours, increasing your movement by an additional 30 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Poison Resistance (-2 Points)
entries: You have resistance to poison damage.

optionalfeature.name: Poison Tolerant (-1 Point)
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against the poisoned condition.

optionalfeature.name: Powerful Build (-2 Points)
entries: You count as one size larger when determining your carrying capacity and the weight you can push, drag, or lift.

optionalfeature.name: Skilled (-2 Points)
entries: You have proficiency with one skill from the following list: Athletics, Perception, Stealth, and Survival.

optionalfeature.name: Small Size (+1 Point)
entries: You are Small instead of Medium.

optionalfeature.name: Speed Increase (-2 Points)
entries: Your walking speed increases by +5. You may purchase this feature up to 3 times.

optionalfeature.name: Sting (-4 Points)
entries: After you hit with an unarmed strike you may inflict an additional 1d6 lightning, necrotic, or poison damage (pick the damage type when you choose your animal of origin). Additionally, if the target fails a Constitution saving throw (DC equal to 12 + your proficiency bonus) they are stunned until the end of the creature's next turn. You may use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. After that you must finish a long rest before using this feature again.

optionalfeature.name: Stoic Dominance
entries: While raging, you have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}. You also automatically succeed on any saving throws to protect against magical illusions.

optionalfeature.name: Stoic Reckoning
entries: When you use Reckless Attack, you gain resistance to one damage type of your choice and are immune to the {@condition stunned} condition until the beginning of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Stoic Strength
entries: You stand firm in the face of danger and use your wits, not your emotions to guide you through battle. As a reaction, when you take damage, you may move up to half your walking speed towards the creature that damaged you and make a melee attack. This movement does not provoke opportunity attacks.

optionalfeature.name: Sunlight Sensitivity (+2 Points)
entries: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on sight-based Wisdom (Perception) checks if you, or the target of your attack (or Perception check) are in direct sunlight.

optionalfeature.name: Swim (-1 Point)
entries: You have a swimming speed equal to double your walking speed.

optionalfeature.name: Swim (-2 Points)
entries: You have a swim speed equal to your walking speed.

optionalfeature.name: Turn of Fate: Dreamer
entries: When you cast your Foresaying spell, roll a {@dice d4}. A result of 4 means that the Foresaying spell is not expended and can be triggered again later.

optionalfeature.name: Turn of Fate: Seeker
entries: You may elect to neutralize a spell. When you or an ally within 10 feet of you are hit by a single target spell you may use a reaction and spend a spell slot of equal (or higher level) to the hostile spell. Doing so causes the spell to fail and have no effect. You must make the decision to use this ability after any saving throws are rolled but before damage is rolled—or the results of the saving throw rolls are revealed.

optionalfeature.name: Unarmored Flight (-4 Points)
entries: You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You cannot wear any armor while flying.

optionalfeature.name: Wild Dominance
entries: While you are raging, you may take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a bonus action. In addition, when you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action this way, moving across {@quickref difficult terrain||3} costs you no extra movement.

optionalfeature.name: Wild Reckoning
entries: While you are raging, you may use an action to make a single melee weapon attack to remove a foe immediately from the battlefield. If you hit, you do maximum damage. Additionally, they must make a Constitution saving throw against a DC of 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. If they fail, they are {@condition stunned} for 1 minute. At the end of each of its turns, the affected creature can make another Constitution saving throw. On a success, the creature is no longer {@condition stunned}.

optionalfeature.name: Wild Strength
entries: No enemy is safe when near you. You may sacrifice an attack and transform it into a wild swing that threatens all opponents within 5 feet of you. Any creature within your weapon's reach must make a Dexterity saving throw against a DC of 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. If they fail, they take damage as though you hit them with an attack made with the weapon used.

optionalfeature.name: Wrathful Dominance
entries: Your proficiency bonus with the {@skill Intimidation} skill is doubled. In addition, while you are {@condition restrained} or {@condition prone}, you do not have disadvantage on melee attacks.

optionalfeature.name: Wrathful Reckoning
entries: When you use Reckless Attack, you may make an additional melee weapon attack as a bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Wrathful Strength
entries: Your fury knows no bounds—when injured you are able to use your attacker's strength to fuel your own. While you are raging, if you take damage from a hostile creature, you may use a reaction to add a provoked point. On future turns you may spend these provoked points to do extra damage when you hit. If you hit a foe, for each provoked point you expend, you gain an extra die of damage. The maximum number of provoked points you may have is equal to your proficiency bonus and all unspent points are lost when your rage ends.

entries.name: Age
entries: You are only a few years old and will age quickly, reaching old age by thirty.

entries.name: Beast Speak
entries: You can communicate with other creatures of your original kind.

entries.name: Unique
entries: There is no one else quite like you in all the world—never has been, never will be. Use the becoming beastborn system below to construct a beastborn of Grimnir.

entries.name: Becoming Beastborn
entries: You start with a pool of 12 beast points.

Work through the following sections to spend these points to acquire the features you believe represent the race you are building. The first step is to decide why you awakened and which beastborn aspects resonate most with you.
Perhaps you are:

items: A boar that seeks out knowledge, collecting and read- ing tomes from across the world, learning as much about the raiders and their past as is possible.
A squirrel who wants to feel the thrill of leading a raiding party across the sea.
A goat that craves to not only learn the culture of the raiders but is excited to add to it—composing great ballads to be sung in feast halls across Grimnir.

entries: Once you have a vision in mind start spending the points.

entries.name: Spending Beastborn Points
entries: In the following descriptions a [-X] means it costs you X points to acquire that feature. A [+X] means you have selected a limitation that adds X points back into your pool.

entries.name: Beast Features
entries: Choose your beast type from these three options: {@filter Hunter & Gatherer|optionalfeatures|source=raidersserpentsea|feature type=b:h&gf}, {@filter Fish|optionalfeatures|source=raidersserpentsea|feature type=b:ff}, and {@filter Fowl|optionalfeatures|source=raidersserpentsea|feature type=b:fof}.

You now spend points to acquire features. Any beastborn can purchase from the {@filter beast features|optionalfeatures|source=raidersserpentsea|feature type=b:bf}, below. You may also purchase from the list associated with your beast type (but not from the lists belonging to other beast types).

entries.name: Age
entries: Grims live longer than humans, up to 250 years.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

entries.name: Amphibious
entries: You can breathe air and water.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You see in {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} within 60 feet of you as if it were {@quickref Vision and Light||2||bright light} and in darkness as if it were {@quickref Vision and Light||2||dim light} (colors in this darkness are seen as shades of gray).

entries.name: Musical
entries: You begin play with proficiency in a {@item musical instrument|PHB} of your choice.

entries.name: Trickster
entries: You know the {@spell thaumaturgy} cantrip.

entries.name: Primal Drives
entries: After each long rest you decide whether you are driven by cruelty or hope. Hopeful grims have the musician features. Cruel grims embrace their bloodthirsty side and have the lurker features. You lose the benefits of the previous drive when you change drives. If you forget to declare your drive, you have the lurker features.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tallfolk age slightly slower than humans, reaching adulthood in their early twenties and living a little past one hundred.

entries.name: Size
entries: Your size is Large.

entries.name: Bulky
entries: Your larger size allows you to double the range of any thrown weapon and add +1 to its damage. Additionally, when you make a melee attack, your reach is increased by 5 feet. All clothing and armor costs 50% more to make.

entries.name: Breaking Point
entries: When hit, you may use your reaction to attack the opponent that damaged you (with advantage). However, using Breaking Point tires you and your initiative is reduced by 10, starting on the next round, for the remainder of this battle. You may use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. After that you must finish a long rest to use this trait again.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tuss age the same as humans.

entries.name: Steadfast
entries: As long as you have not taken lightning damage since your last turn, if you are reduced to zero hit points, or as a bonus action on your turn, you may spend one Hit Die to regain hit points. Roll your die and add your Constitution modifier. You are healed by this amount. You are unable to use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: True Nature
entries: As an action, you manifest your true nature. Your physical appearance changes noticeably. Your skin or eyes may shift to an unusual color. You might sprout horns or suddenly become covered with fur or scales. Define these appearance details when you select this race. You may use an action to hide your true nature once more.
